name: Cheryl's Sourdough Starter
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the water, milk, sugar, and salt.
2. Add the flour gradually until a shaggy dough forms.
3. Cover loosely or wrap in Saran Wrap and let sit at room temperature for 3 days.
4. On the third day, stir the mixture well.
5. Let it sit uncovered for an additional 2 days.
6. Optionally, add dissolved yeast (1 packet mixed with ½ cup warm water) on the fifth day to speed up souring. If added, let it sit another week, stirring daily.
7. If not using the added yeast, let it sit a full week.
8. Store in the refrigerator. The mixture will separate into liquid and solids; stir the liquid back into the solid part before use.
9. To use: Discard the desired amount of starter.
10. Feed with equal parts flour (½ cup) and sugar (1 teaspoon).
11. Stir until well combined, allowing for lumps.
12. Let it sit at room temperature uncovered overnight.
13. Stir again and return to the refrigerator.
14. Wait at least 3-4 days before using if not used within a week; feed with 1 teaspoon of sugar, stir, and return to the fridge.
(Note: Cheryl's Sourdough Starter is considered one of the best.)
